tool_args.label: Plan balanced only with federal funding
tool_args.raw_text: While the Plan is balanced through 2025, it is only balanced with $10.5 billion in federal funding through CRRSAA and ARPA, as well as with the $4.0 billion in CARES Act funding that was received and used to balance the 2020 budget.

payload.claim_text: The Plan is balanced through 2025 only with $10.5 billion in federal funding through CRRSAA and ARPA, plus $4.0 billion in CARES Act funding received and used to balance the 2020 budget. Without fare and toll increases, federal funding, and deficit borrowing proceeds, each year would be substantially out of balance: deficits of $3.4 billion in 2021, $2.5 billion in 2022, $2.0 billion in 2023, $2.5 billion in 2024, and $3.2 billion in 2025.

tool_args.label: Structural imbalance masked by federal funding
tool_args.raw_text: The federal funding, however, masks the structural imbalance in MTA's finances. Without the fare and toll increases, ARPA and the use of deficit borrowing proceeds, 2025 would be $3.2 billion out of balance. Even if the fare and toll increases were implemented, 2025 would still be out of balance by $2.4 billion without federal funding and the deficit borrowing proceeds. And with federal funding exhausted in 2025 and only $1.5 billion remaining in deficit borrowing proceeds, 2026 will likely be significantly out of balance without actions to address the structural imbalance.

payload.claim_text: Federal funding masks the structural imbalance in MTA's finances. Without fare and toll increases, ARPA, and deficit borrowing proceeds, 2025 would be $3.2 billion out of balance. Even with fare/toll increases, 2025 would be $2.4 billion out of balance without federal funding and deficit borrowing proceeds. With federal funding exhausted in 2025 and $1.5 billion remaining in deficit borrowing, 2026 will likely be significantly out of balance.

tool_args.label: Ridership recovery percentages Nov 2021
tool_args.raw_text: As of the first week in November, ridership recovery as a percentage of pre-pandemic levels was 55 percent on Subways, 64 percent on Buses, 40 percent on SIR, 52 percent on LIRR and 48 percent on MNR. Traffic on B&T crossings was at 97 percent of the pre-pandemic crossing level.

payload.claim_text: As of first week of November 2021, ridership recovery as percentage of pre-pandemic levels: Subways 55%, Buses 64%, SIR 40%, LIRR 52%, MNR 48%. B&T traffic at 97% of pre-pandemic level.

tool_args.label: McKinsey new normal ridership projections
tool_args.raw_text: The McKinsey projections anticipate a 'new normal' ridership level of between 82 percent and 91 percent of pre-pandemic levels by the end of the first quarter of 2024.

payload.claim_text: McKinsey projections anticipate a 'new normal' ridership level of between 82% and 91% of pre-pandemic levels by end of first quarter of 2024, driven by hybrid work schedules, increased online shopping, slower tourism return, and increases in alternative travel like walking and bicycling. B&T traffic expected to fully recover to pre-pandemic level by end of Q2 2022.

tool_args.label: 2021 fare increase deferred to mid-2022
tool_args.raw_text: The 2021 fare increase was originally proposed to go into effect in March 2021, and then deferred in the July Plan until November 2021. In the November Plan, this action is proposed to be implemented in mid-2022 and this timing change reduces the farebox revenue that is expected to be generated by $17 million in 2021 and $88 million in 2022.

payload.claim_text: The 2021 fare increase deferred from March 2021 to November 2021 in July Plan, then further deferred to mid-2022 in November Plan, reducing farebox revenue by $17 million in 2021 and $88 million in 2022.

tool_args.label: Service guideline changes and wage freeze removed
tool_args.raw_text: With the MTA welcoming riders back to the subway, bus and commuter railroads, and with finances clearer in the near term thanks to the infusion of $14.5 billion in federal funding, the proposed service guideline-based changes and the proposed two-year wage freeze are removed from this Plan, eliminating savings of almost $1.4 billion through 2025 that had been included in the July Plan.

payload.claim_text: Proposed service guideline-based changes and proposed two-year wage freeze removed from November Plan due to $14.5 billion federal funding infusion, eliminating savings of almost $1.4 billion through 2025 that had been included in the July Plan.

tool_args.label: Zero-emissions fleet transition by 2040
tool_args.raw_text: expansion of the all-electric bus fleet testing program as the MTA transitions to a fully zero-emissions fleet by 2040

payload.claim_text: MTA is transitioning to a fully zero-emissions fleet by 2040, including expansion of the all-electric bus fleet testing program.

tool_args.label: Farebox Operating Ratio - about half
tool_args.raw_text: combined fares and tolls only cover approximately half of operating costs ("Farebox Operating Ratio") and a little more than a third of total expenses, including capital costs ("Farebox Recovery Ratio")

payload.claim_text: Combined fares and tolls cover approximately half of operating costs (Farebox Operating Ratio) and a little more than a third of total expenses including capital costs (Farebox Recovery Ratio).

tool_args.label: Central Business District Tolling Program
tool_args.raw_text: Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP), which aims to reduce congestion in Manhattan's Central Business District (CBD) and, along with other revenues in the Capital Lockbox, will generate the revenue necessary to support $15 billion in bonding capacity for the 2020-2024 and subsequent MTA Capital Programs.

payload.description: Program to reduce congestion in Manhattan's Central Business District via electronic tolling; authorized by MTA Reform and Traffic Mobility Act, enacted April 2019. Includes installation of electronic tolling equipment on the perimeter and within the CBD, defined as streets south and inclusive of 60th Street in Manhattan, excluding FDR Drive, NYS Route 9A/West Side Highway, Battery Park Underpass, and surface road portion of Hugh L Carey Tunnel. Environmental Assessment (EA) underway per FHWA guidance. FHWA decision anticipated in late 2022.
